headline: Tesla Investor Ross Gerber After Getting A New Rivian R1S: 'I'M Bored Of The Model Y'
summary: Ross Gerber, the president and CEO of Gerber Kawasaki Wealth and Investment Management and a Tesla Inc (NASDAQ: TSLA) bull and investor, said on Sunday that he is 'bored' of the company's Model Y SUV.

headline: Tesla Hits 20K+ Registrations In Sweden As Ongoing Workers' Strike Nears 2 Months
summary: EV giant Tesla Inc said on Sunday that over 20k of its cars have been registered in Sweden this year.

headline: Nvidia And Employees Give $15M To Israeli Non-Profits Amid Israel-Hamas Conflict
summary: Nvidia has contributed $15 million to Israeli nonprofits to aid civilians impacted by the ongoing conflict with Hamas.

headline: Google Restricts Election-Related Queries, Bill Gates Enthused By AI Innovations And More: This Week In Artificial Intelligence
summary: The week was once again a thrilling one for technology enthusiasts, especially for those keeping tabs on the evolving AI landscape.

headline: Apple Executives Spill The Beans: Here's Why The iPhone Maker's Chips Outshine Rivals
summary: From just one product in 2010, Apple's chips now power everything from iPhones, iPads, Macs and even accessories like AirPods.

headline: Alphabet Dodges Multi-Billion Dollar Blow By Rejecting Google Play Fee Model Change: Epic Lawsuit Documents Reveal
summary: Google reportedly rejected plans to change its Play Store fee structure based on a projected loss of billions of dollars, according to Epic Games lawsuit.

headline: Apple's $17B Smartwatch Business At Risk, Cutting-Edge AI Tech 'HUGS' And More: This Week In Appleverse
summary: From threats to its smartwatch operations to a significant increase in its card savings interest rate, let's delve into the Apple-centric news that unfolded this past week.
